ignore.reason: The narrator of this story writes in an idiosyncratic style. Adding the commas to numbers would add enough polish to meaningfully alter his characterization.
This letter is written in an idiosyncratic style. Lowercase letters start sentences intentionally.
This story is written in an idiosyncratic style by a barely literate narrator. The incorrectly formatted initialisms are intentional
The hyphen is at the end of the line on purpose. It moves the second half of the word to the next line to preserve poetic rhyme and rhythm.
The name of the newspaper has a possessive, so it belongs in the italics.
This appears to be a second paragraph from the same speaker. This is grammatically correct.
The title of a newspaper is embedded in these chapter titles, and so "The" is uppercase.
This story is written in an idiosyncratic style. Lowercase letters start sentences and paragraphs intentionally
This story is written in an idiosyncratic style by a barely literate narrator. The incorrectly formatted initialisms are intentional
This story is written in an idiosyncratic style. Lowercase letters start sentences and paragraphs intentionally
This story is written in an idiosyncratic style by a barely literate narrator. The incorrectly capitalization of the ship name is intentional.
This story is written in an idiosyncratic style by a barely literate narrator. The single letters here are not graphemes or phonemes, just intentionally bad grammar.
The question mark is inside the italics tag because the question mark is part of the title of the play.
The first single quote is an elision character, so having it duplicated is fine here.
The question mark is inside the italics tag because the question mark is part of the title of the play.
